message:

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "vllm-1002-gguf-support",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "vllm: GGUF support",
+    "description": "Enable GGUF model format support in vLLM for flexible quantization control. GGUF allows fine-grained control over bit depth and VRAM usage compared to other quantization formats like AWQ. Requested by 56+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current vLLM setup",
+        "description": "Verify your vLLM version and configuration:\n```bash\npip show vllm | grep Version\n# or\npython -c \"import vllm; print(vllm.__version__)\"\n```\nGGUF support requires vLLM v0.3.0 or later."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Install or upgrade vLLM with GGUF support",
+        "description": "Ensure you have a version of vLLM that includes GGUF support:\n```bash\npip install vllm --upgrade\n```\nvLLM's GGUF support loads quantized weights and dequantizes them to fp16 for matrix multiplication. This gives you control over VRAM usage by choosing different quantization bit depths (e.g., Q2_K, Q4_K, Q5_K)."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Obtain a GGUF model",
+        "description": "Download a pre-quantized GGUF model from HuggingFace or convert one yourself:\n```bash\n# Example: convert a GGUF file to a torch-compatible format\nPROTOCOL_BUFFERS_PYTHON_IMPLEMENTATION=python python convert_gguf_to_torch.py \\\n  --input mixtral-8x7b-instruct-v0.1.Q2_K.gguf \\\n  --output mixtral-q2k\n```\nMany popular models are available in GGUF format on HuggingFace (e.g., TheBloke's quantized models)."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Serve the GGUF model with vLLM",
+        "description": "Start the vLLM OpenAI-compatible API server with a GGUF model:\n```bash\npython -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server \\\n  --model <path-to-gguf-model> \\\n  --tokenizer <original-model-name>\n```\nThe GGUF weights are dequantized to fp16 at load time, then standard vLLM inference runs on the dequantized weights."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that GGUF model serving is working:\n```bash\ncurl http://localhost:8000/v1/completions \\\n  -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" \\\n  -d '{\"model\": \"<model-name>\", \"prompt\": \"Hello, world!\", \"max_tokens\": 50}'\n```\nYou should receive a valid completion response from the GGUF-quantized model."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "vLLM added GGUF format support by implementing weight loading that dequantizes GGUF quantized weights to fp16 for matrix multiplication. This enables users to run GGUF-quantized models with vLLM's high-throughput serving engine while maintaining control over VRAM usage via quantization bit depth selection.",
+      "codeSnippets": [
+        "# Serve a GGUF model with vLLM\npython -m vllm.entrypoints.openai.api_server --model <path-to-gguf-model>",
+        "# Convert GGUF to torch format\nPROTOCOL_BUFFERS_PYTHON_IMPLEMENTATION=python python convert_gguf_to_torch.py --input model.Q2_K.gguf --output model-q2k"
+      ]
+    }
+  },
+  "metadata": {
+    "tags": [
+      "vllm",
+      "community",
+      "llm-serving",
+      "feature",
+      "gguf",
+      "quantization"
+    ],
+    "cncfProjects": [
+      "vllm"
+    ],
+    "targetResourceKinds": [],
+    "difficulty": "advanced",
+    "issueTypes": [
+      "feature"
+    ],
+    "maturity": "community",
+    "sourceUrls": {
+      "issue": "https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/issues/1002",
+      "repo": "https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm"
+    },
+    "reactions": 56,
+    "comments": 49,
+    "synthesizedBy": "copilot"
+  },
+  "prerequisites": {
+    "tools": [
+      "python",
+      "pip"
+    ],
+    "description": "Python 3.8+, pip, a CUDA-capable GPU, and a working vLLM installation (pip install vllm)."
+  },
+  "security": {
+    "scannedAt": "2026-03-12T06:44:42.325Z",
+    "scannerVersion": "cncf-gen-3.0.0",
+    "sanitized": true,
+    "findings": []
+  }
+}
